rulings.Case Title: DAVID APPELBAUM VS. PACIFIC GAS AND ELECTRIC COMPANY ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Rescind Settlement Agreement (Per Order Filed 11/14/18)
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, November 28, 2018, Line 3, PLAINTIFF DAVID APPELBAUM's Motion To Rescind Settlement Agreement (Per Order Filed 11/14/18) Plaintiff David Appelbaum's motion to rescind settlement agreement is denied. Mr. Applebaum has not shown that there has been a material failure of consideration. Defendant Pacific Gas and Electric Company was required to withhold taxes from the amount payable to Mr. Appelbaum and remit the withholding to the state and federal taxing authorities. Withholding of taxes was also contemplated by the parties per the express language of the settlement agreement. Any error by PG&E in the calculation of the amounts withheld which resulted in overwitholding does not constitute a material failure of consideration and Mr. Appelbaum's remedy is to seek refunds. (Rivera v. Baker West, Inc. (9th Cir. 2005) 430 F.3d 1253, 1260 ("Rivera is not without recourse, either as to the fact or the amount of income tax withheld. He remains free to seek a refund for wrongfully withheld taxes via a direct claim to the Internal Revenue Service."); Cifuentes v. Costco Wholesale Corp. (2015) 238 Cal.App.4th 65, 80 (plaintiff's "remedy was to seek refunds for any excess withholding, not further damages from" the defendant)). Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for PG&E is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/hk

rulings.Case Title: KAMBIZ ALI KAZEMI VS. TIPSY PIG ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Compel Further Response To Discovery And For A Monetary Sanction
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, November 28, 2018, Line 2, DEFENDANT OUR NEIGHBORHOOD PLACE, LLC'S Motion To Compel Further Response To Discovery And For A Monetary Sanction Pro Tem Judge James Fleming, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: The motion will be denied because plaintiff's responses, as amended, are adequate. Plaintiff is under no obligation to obtain documents from third-party employers or medical providers to satisfy defendant's document demands. Further, the discovery department is not the appropriate venue in which to litigate the veracity of plaintiff's discovery responses. Finally, Request for Production No. 7 was unintelligible and unenforceable. Sanctions are denied. The parties are advised to adhere to the Local Rules concerning submission of courtesy copies of motion papers and to promptly correct any deficiencies in that regard. Responding party shall submit a proposed order at the hearing as well as a Word version thereof via email to jameshfleming@gmail.com. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to jameshfleming@gmail.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

No opposition filed. If Mr. Boreen files an amended complaint prior to the hearing the demurrer will be taken off calendar. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling adversely affects, in whole or in part, any party who is not represented by an attorney, then any other party (whether represented by an attorney or not) may submit on the tentative ruling without appearing for the hearing only if the adversely affected self-represented party has stated in writing or orally that it, he or she will not appear at the hearing. Counsel for the City is required to prepare a proposed order which states that the demurrer was not opposed and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/hk

rulings.Case Title: ELECTRICAL INDUSTRY SERVICE BUREAU, INC. VS. THE TRAVELERS INDEMNITY COMPANY ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings On Electrical Industry Service Bureaus First Amended Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, November 28, 2018, Line 6, DEFENDANT THE TRAVELERS INDEMNITY COMPANY's Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings On Electrical Industry Service Bureaus First Amended Complaint Defendant The Travelers Indemnity Company's motion for judgment on the pleadings on the sole remaining cause of action for violation of the Unfair Competition Law in the first amended complaint filed by plaintiff Electrical Industry Service Bureau, Inc. is granted without leave to amend. EISB has not alleged any facts nor does it appear that it can allege any facts entitling it to restitution, the only monetary relief available on a UCL claim. EISB has not alleged, nor does it appear that it is able to allege, that it provided anything of value to Travelers. The case law cited by EISB is inapplicable because the plaintiffs in those cases conferred some form of benefit on the defendants for which plaintiffs were never compensated. Moreover, Moradi-Shalal v. Fireman's Fund Insurance Companies (1988) 46 Cal.3d 287, 292 precludes a UCL claim based on Insurance Code 790.03. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for Travelers is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/hk

The Court further finds that defendant Anderson's original verification of his requests for admissions mistakenly referred to defendant Anderson as "plaintiff". Unless the same have already been accomplished, responding party is granted ten days leave pursuant to Code of Civil Procedure Section 473 to serve amended versions of said documents correcting the mistaken references described above. The motion is otherwise denied. Sanctions are denied. The parties are reminded that minor errors can and should be resolved by informal meet and confer proceedings without the necessity of court intervention. Responding party shall submit a proposed order at the hearing as well as a Word version thereof via email to jameshfleming@gmail.com. rulings.Case Title: JAMES DALESSANDRO ET AL VS. STEPHENS INSTITUTE, A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Arbitration And To Stay All Proceedings As To Plaintiffs Dalessandro, Louria, Byars, Braverman And Corson; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities In Support Thereof
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, November 28, 2018, Line 9, DEFENDANT STEPHENS INSTITUTE D/B/A ACADEMY OF ART UNIVERSITY's Motion To Compel Arbitration And To Stay All Proceedings As To Plaintiffs Dalessandro, Louria, Byars, Braverman And Corson; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities In Support Thereof Defendant Stephens Institute's motion to compel arbitration as to all plaintiffs and to stay this case pending the conclusion of arbitration proceedings is granted. Because plaintiffs' claims relate to their employment by AAU, the expiration of the employment agreements do not render the arbitration agreements inapplicable. Per federal and California case law, because the parties' arbitration agreements clearly and unmistakably delegate the threshold question of arbitrability to the arbitrator, all issues regarding arbitrability must be decided by the arbitrator. Plaintiffs do not advance any argument that the delegation clauses, as distinguished from the arbitration language, are unconscionable or otherwise unenforceable. Plaintiffs' arguments regarding the unconscionability of the arbitration agreements, including any arguments that any of the plaintiffs opted out or were deterred from opting out of those agreements, must be heard and decided by the arbitrator. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for AAU is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/hk
